title: Algorithms & Data Struc I
description: Object-oriented design: objects, classes, methods, encapsulation; programming fundamentals: data, variables, selection, loops, arrays, input/output; exceptions.

objectives: Establish an understanding of fundamental object-oriented software concepts, including objects, classes, and encapsulation.
Establish an understanding of core programming concepts, including algorithms, data storage, control structures, and method invocation.
Students should be able to design and implement a small application that uses library classes and one or two student-created classes.
Establish a fundamental proficiency in programming using the Java programming language.

textbook.title: Rephactor
textbook.description: Interactive learning for Java programming practice, algorithmic problem-solving, and formative feedback.

classSchedule.topic: Introduction to Java programming and how computers process instructions
Fundamentals of algorithms, data and variables, and arithmetic expressions
Working with strings and building interactive programs using the Scanner class
Designing conditional algorithms with boolean expressions, operators, and if statements
Building repetition algorithms with while loops, break and continue flow control
Exam 1 covering Chapters 1 through 4 and core programming foundations
Extending repetition and conditional algorithm design through applied exercises
Introduction to objects and classes, including writing classes - part 1
Understanding Java APIs (Random and Math classes) and number systems
Continuing objects and classes with writing classes - part 2 and practice
Exam 2 covering Chapters 5 through 8 and object-oriented concepts
One-class module on array data structures and element access patterns
Advanced array data-structure techniques and introduction to list abstractions
File input and output workflows, plus basic exception-handling patterns
